IS

Company configuration, lot management & Tally exports — built for the cardamom trade.

  • Trade-aware invoicing & debit notes
  • ISP / ASP dual-identity support
  • One-click Tally voucher export
  • Role-based access for your team

💾 Backup & Restore

Download the entire database as a single .db file, or restore from one you saved earlier. A backup captures every auction, lot, invoice, payment, debit note, setting and user — bit-for-bit identical to what's running right now.

Download Backup
Saves a timestamped spice-etrade-backup-YYYY-MM-DD…db file to your downloads folder. Take one before any major change, or on a regular schedule.
⏰Auto Backup Schedule Off
Automatically saves a snapshot to data/backups/auto-backup-…db on the server, on the cadence you choose. Old auto backups beyond the retention count are removed. Run while the app is running.
backups
📁 Recent auto backups
No auto backups yet.
⚠️Restore from Backup
Replaces ALL current data with the contents of the uploaded file. Anything not in the backup will be lost. Download a backup first if you want a recovery point.
🗑Delete All — wipe an entire table
Transactional wipes (Invoices, Purchases, Bills, Debit Notes, auctions) only remove records for the current business mode — an e-Auction wipe never touches e-Trade data and vice versa. Sellers & Buyers are master data shared by both modes, so wiping them affects e-Trade and e-Auction alike; the Everything reset wipes the whole database across both modes. The server snapshots the database to data/backups/before-delete-…<timestamp>.db first, so a misclick is recoverable via Restore above. Every wipe is audit-logged.
📜 Audit log — recent wipes
Shows the 200 most recent Delete All actions.
Click Refresh to load recent wipes.

🛡App Activity Log

Every create / edit / delete across the whole app — the office desktop and the field-staff mobile app — including invoices, sellers, buyers, users and settings.
Click Refresh to load app activity.
▾ Users & Access

Create separate logins for each person. Each user can stay signed in on their own phone/computer without logging the others out.

UsernameRoleActive SessionsLast ActiveCreatedActions

Every place you're signed in as . Revoke any old/unused session to secure your account.

▾ Change My Password

Changing your password signs you out from all other devices (this one stays signed in).

Add New User

Create a login for another person (e.g. your dad, or a branch operator). They'll use it from their own device.

3–30 characters · letters, digits, . _ - only
Share this with the person — they can change it later from their account.
Can create/edit lots, generate invoices, and manage buyers/traders. Cannot delete records or change settings.

Reset User Password

Resetting 's password will immediately sign them out from all devices.

Change Role

Update 's permissions. The change takes effect on their next request — they don't need to sign out.

Extra Permissions

Grant capabilities on top of their role. Takes effect on their next action — they don't need to sign out.

▼ Export Center

▼ Pre-Trade Exports "Before trade" snapshots — your record of the pre-trade state

Download these before importing prices. Once prices are imported the lots table is overwritten with the auctioneer's data, so these are the only record of the pre-trade state. Uses the auction selected in the top bar.

▼ DBF Exports FoxPro-compatible format for legacy integration

These exports produce .dbf files that your dad's FoxPro application can read directly (master data — Sellers / Buyers — also exports as .xlsx). Date format: DD/MM/YYYY. Lot numbers preserve leading zeros.

Lorry Reports Auction-floor reports for the day's auction

Note: All three reports pull live data from the selected auction's lots and the buyers master. The Buyer Lot Lorry report classifies buyers as Intra-state when the buyer's state matches the auction state, otherwise Inter-state.

To Tally Generate Tally-importable XML files from your auction data Tally Cloud

Two Tally companies for ledgers: Sales Party Ledgers import into the ISP Tally company (since buyers are ISP customers). RD & URD Purchase Party Ledgers import into the ASP Tally company (since suppliers are ASP's). All vouchers currently import into ISP — open the matching company in Tally before importing.
Workflow: Import party ledgers first, then matching vouchers — Tally rejects imports if any referenced ledger is missing.

▼ 📚 Party Ledgers 3

🛒
Sales Party Ledgers ISP
Buyers from this auction's invoices (under Local / Inter Dealer-Purchase)
🏢
RD Purchase Party Ledgers ASP
Registered dealers (under Local / Inter Dealer)
🌾
URD Purchase Party Ledgers ASP
Agriculturists (under Purchase From Agriculturist)

▼ 🎯 Single Party Ledger

Pick one party from this auction and download just its ledger. Useful when you've added a new buyer/trader and only need to create that one ledger in Tally.
🔍
Pick an auction and click Refresh List to load parties.

▼ 🎫 Single Voucher

One voucher per party (covers all their lots) — for a missed entry or a re-import.
🔍
Pick an auction and click Refresh List to load parties.
🔍
Pick an auction + voucher type and click Load Invoices.

▼ 📄 Vouchers 7

💰
Sales Vouchers — ISP ISP
ISP → outside customers — full e-way bill, dispatch from ASP, BASICORDERREF
🔁
Sales Vouchers — ASP ASP
ASP → ISP internal transfer — uses ASP planter rates (asp_puramt)
📦
ISP Purchase Vouchers ISP
Mirror of ASP→ISP transfer — same voucher number (ASP/I-…), supplier=ASP, IGST input
📥
RD Purchase Vouchers ASP
Registered dealers — input GST, TDS 194Q, batch tracking
🌾
URD Purchase Vouchers ASP
Agriculturist / unregistered — Nil Rated, no GST input
📝
Debit Notes ASP
Discount received from suppliers — 18% GST credit
🌱
Debit Notes - Planter ASP
Discount on planter bills of supply — commission/handling, 18% GST credit

▼ 🗺️ E-way Bill Distance Per-invoice override

ISP sales vouchers carry a <DISTANCE> field used by the e-way bill portal. Click NIC to look up the distance on the official portal (captcha-gated; one route at a time), paste the result, click Save. The value is saved per route, so every other invoice between the same two PINs — this auction and all future ones — picks it up automatically. The auto badge marks rows that resolved via the saved route table.
Pick an auction and click Refresh.
How to import into Tally:
  1. Open your Tally company (must match the name in Settings → To Tally).
  2. Press Alt+O → Import → Vouchers.
  3. Select the downloaded .xml file and confirm.
  4. Tally rejects the import if any ledger name in the XML doesn't exist in your company — make sure the ledgers in Settings → To Tally match your Tally setup exactly.

Journals Sales & Purchase registers

Registers Lot-wise Purchase & invoice-wise Sales — by trade or date range

Pick a Trade for a single trade, or leave it on “All trades” and set a From/To date range. The date range is ignored when a specific trade is selected.

Reports — Spice Board

Statutory cardamom-auction reports submitted to the Spices Board (Cardamom Marketing Rules).

Import Old Data Upload legacy XLS/XLSX exports back into the system

Price List Mapping Upload a Price List (Before) sheet with TRADE NAME filled in — server fills the CODE column from the Buyers master

Expected columns: AUCTION NO, DATE, LOT, BAG, QTY, CODE, TRADE NAME  ·  The matcher needs both TRADE NAME and CODE headers somewhere on the sheet. Extra columns are kept as-is.

Price Check Reconcile an external price sheet against a trade's saved lot ledger

Pick a trade, upload the external price sheet, then click Verify Prices. The first sheet is used. Columns recognised (case-insensitive): Lot No, Price, optional Buyer Code. When the DB price is correct and the file is wrong, use Keep DB to acknowledge the row without overwriting the lot.

New Auction

Will be tagged as —. Locked at creation — switch business mode in Settings before saving if needed.

Lots for —

Lot Allocations — Auction #

Click any free chip to mark it for deletion. Used (entered) chips are locked. To add a new range, use + Add Branch.

★ Main depot Closing a depot pushes its remaining free lots here so they can be re-allocated.
🔁 Auto-fill from existing lots — Use this when a trade was created via import (or otherwise has lots without matching allocations). One click adds a wide range (e.g. 001–999) per branch + lot-prefix combination found in the lots table, so every existing lot has a branch home. Safe to re-run: anything already covered is skipped.

Move unused lot numbers from one branch to another. Click a tile to toggle it, or drag across tiles to select a whole range — drag again elsewhere to add another range (e.g. 001-010 and 090-100). Booked lots (with sellers) cannot be reassigned.

+ Add Extra Lots

Add a new lot range to the current trade. Existing allocations are preserved.

Edit Lot

Change Seller

Type a few letters to search…
Picking a seller replaces the lot's name, CR/GSTIN, PAN, phone, Aadhar and address fields. Click Save on the lot edit form to persist.

Generate Sales Invoice(s)

Select an auction first

Generate Purchase Invoice

Only for sellers with GSTIN (registered dealers).

Generate All

When set, only buyers whose default sale type matches are included.
Each generated invoice increments by 1 from here

Commission Bill (Format 2)

Generates a consolidated Commission Bill (Format 2) for the selected auction — one row per seller listing Amount, Refund, Commission, Handling, GST and net dues.

Generate Agriculturist Bill of Supply

Only for sellers without GSTIN (farmers, agriculturists). No GST is charged. Sellers with GSTIN should use Generate Purchase Invoice instead.

Generate Debit Note

Generates one debit note for the specified purchase invoice. The invoice must belong to the selected trade.

—

Generate All Debit Notes

Generates one debit note per eligible purchase in the selected trade. Purchases already having a debit note are skipped automatically. No cross-trade data — only purchases under this trade are touched.

—
Loading eligible purchases…

Generate Debit Note - Planter

Generates one debit note for the specified bill of supply. The bill must belong to the selected trade.

—

Generate All Debit Notes - Planter

Generates one debit note per eligible bill of supply in the selected trade. Bills already having a debit note are skipped automatically. No cross-trade data — only bills under this trade are touched.

—
Loading eligible bills of supply…

Invoice Preview — not yet saved

Import Lots from XLS / XLSX

Import lots from an Excel file. The file should have columns like ANO (trade no), DATE, LOT, NAME, BAG, QTY, etc.

💡 If your file has multiple trade numbers (ANO 1, 2, 3…), leave the override fields below blank — each row goes into its own auction automatically.

Set Buyer for selected lots

No lots selected. Trade name and sale type auto-fill from the Buyers master.

Price Import — Post-Auction Data

⚠ Download "before trade" snapshots first
Once you import prices the lots table is overwritten with the auctioneer's data. These reports are your record of the pre-trade state. Snapshots use the trade picked in the topbar.

Use this after the auction ends to update prices, buyer codes, and quantities on existing lots.

Expected columns: STATE, TNO (or ANO), DATE, LOT, BAG, QTY, PRICE, CODE
Extra columns are ignored. Amount is auto-computed as QTY × PRICE if not provided.
🚫 Validate entered lots first
This trade's entered lots haven't been validated. Run validation and resolve any issues before importing prices.

Validate Entered Lots
